input.background: Suppose we treat four patients with an intervention that is successful 75% of the time. The number of successes will vary randomly. Occasionally, no patients will respond; sometimes one will respond; and so on.
input.question: Figure 5.2 displays it in graphical form.

Number_of_successes.description: The number of successful interventions out of four patients.

Probability.description: The probability of achieving a certain number of successes in the intervention.

meta_info.caption: Example of a probability mass function (The “four  patients” illustrative example).
